title: spex frame-sequence manifest (sequence.json)
description: Written by `spex frame-sequence` (crates/spex-cli/src/frame_sequence.rs): describes a real multi-frame point-cloud animation — N real point-cloud files, each tiled into its own real octree tileset (via spex_tiler::build_with_offset) under a frame-NNN/ subdirectory, all sharing one coordinate offset so the viewer can swap between them without the point cloud's position jumping. Read by the viewer (viewer/src/tileset.ts's fetchSequence) to drive real frame-advance playback — absent for every other tileset (a plain spex convert/graph-layout output has no sequence.json at all).

frameCount.description: Equal to frames.length — the real number of frame-NNN/ tileset subdirectories written alongside this manifest.

fps.description: Real playback rate the viewer advances frames at (not a rendering frame rate — the WebGL render loop itself still runs as fast as the browser allows).

items.description: A directory name, relative to this manifest's own directory, containing a real tileset.json + octree/*.bin — fetchable the normal way via fetchTileset/fetchNodePoints at `${baseUrl}/${frame}`.
